message:
feat(connectors/anthropic): expose SSE event parser via __fuzz + register fuzz target

Promote the Anthropic SSE plumbing that the existing `__fuzz` module
needed to actually exercise the streaming envelope:

- `client::MAX_SSE_BUFFER_BYTES` and `client::parse_sse_event_bytes`
  go from private to `pub(crate)` so the fuzz seam can reach them.
- `lib.rs`'s `__fuzz` module gains `parse_sse_event_bytes_fuzz`
  (driving the internal event parser) and a re-exported
  `FUZZ_MAX_SSE_BUFFER_BYTES` constant so the harness can hit the
  oversized-buffer rejection boundary without hard-coding the cap.
  The doc comment enumerates the five behaviors the fuzz target is
  expected to cover (oversize rejection, invalid UTF-8 rejection,
  data-line joining, unknown-event-type skipping, envelope/payload
  type mismatch).

Plus the matching fuzz target itself
(`fuzz/fuzz_targets/anthropic_sse_envelope.rs`) and its `[[bin]]`
registration in `fuzz/Cargo.toml` (`fuzz_anthropic_sse_envelope`),
keeping the existing convention of `fuzz_<name>` binary naming and
explicit `test/doc/bench = false` for cargo-fuzz compatibility.

Tracking: bead `flywheel_connectors-dzveq`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1211,5 +1211,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_anthropic_sse_envelope"
+path = "fuzz_targets/anthropic_sse_envelope.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
